affected_groups: village communities facing imminent viability thresholds
content.decision_question: Should Hungary delay or sequence structural reforms behind the establishment of a viability registry and cost-transparency framework, or proceed with structural interventions (T1-T5) now given the pace of demographic contraction, treating evidence-building as a parallel rather than prerequisite process?
content.evidence_boundary: Evidence can eventually show (retrospectively) whether earlier structural action or earlier evidence-building produced better outcomes, and can quantify the pace of cohort collapse against implementation timelines. It cannot resolve, in advance, whether political actors will actually use the registry's findings rather than override them, since this depends on future political will rather than any measurable fact currently available.
content.tension: T6 proposes a national viability registry and cost-visibility framework to inform decisions across all structural models, but building this evidence infrastructure takes time during which the demographic cliff (cohort collapse, zero-enrollment cliffs) continues, and Hungary's history shows top-down political decisions have repeatedly overridden technical evidence (as in Poland 2017), raising the question of whether investing in transparency now is worth delaying structural action.
content.title: Acting now under demographic pressure versus building evidence infrastructure first
value_poles: urgency of responding to accelerating demographic decline
